ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Continued To Jun-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw appeared by zoom for and with Lucinda Lee by zoom.

The order to appear is off calendar. The court stated that it did not find a statement of good cause for Mrs. Hinshaw's failure to appear and orders sanctions in the amount of $250.00 to be paid within seven days. Mrs. Hinshaw stated that the first and final accounting of the original administrator is pending in the court's e-file queue, that Mrs. Lee's petition to increase bond has been accepted, and that she believes Mrs. Lee needed to have the original administrator's accounting settled before she could finish things. The court noted that the accounting petition has been filed with a hearing date of May 22nd. Status on close of estate is continued to June 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody./Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Continued To Mar-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any party or counsel.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Successor Administrator Lucinda Lee and her attorney Caroline Hinshaw to appear on March 13, 2023, at 9:00 a.m.,in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. In addition, an Order to Show Cause will be issued to attorney Caroline Hinshaw to appear and explain why $250.00 sanctions should not be imposed for her failure to appear. The status hearings are also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Resigning Co-Administrators Final Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-28-2022 Continued To Jan-30-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw appeared by zoom for Emily Cohen.

Mrs. Hinshaw apologized to the court for not appearing at the last hearing and assured the court that it won't happen again. The order to appear is off calendar. Mrs. Hinshaw also stated that there is a group called FAS which is a fiduciary in accounting services, that they have submitted the statements to them, that she received the accounting last Tuesday and she returned it back to them, but they were closed, and that there are some changes that need to be made to the accounting, but they got good progress. The court offered a continuance date in January. Mrs. Hinshaw stated that the accounting is going to be done much earlier than that, that she needs to get it off her desk because they also have to get Mrs. Lee's accounting done, and that the estate is ready to be closed and she anticipates filing a final petition. After further discussion, status on filing of the resigning co-administrator's final accounting is continued to January 30, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, and the status hearing on close of estate currently on calendar for December 21st, is also continued to January 30, 2023.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-17-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, via Zoom, for and with Lucinda Lee, Petitioner, and for and with Emily and Maireid Cohen, suspended Co-Administrators.

The court advises that it is prepared to accept the resignation of the suspended co-Administrators, but it is not willing to waive the accounting. The court and Ms. Hinshaw discuss the VOYA account listed as a cash payout and the court advises Ms. Hinshaw that it would like an update regarding the VOYA account and a date of death statement for the same. Ms. Hinshaw reports that the account offered an option to take a cash payout. The court withdraws its request for the date of death statement. The court advises that it is ready to appoint Ms. Lee as successor Administrator but will require a full bond of $7 million dollars.

After additional discussion, the Order to Show Cause is taken off calendar. The Petition for Letters of Administration is taken under submission. A Status of Filing of Resigning Co-Administrators Final Accounting is set for Oct-17-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jul-25-2022 Continued To Sep-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, for and with Emily Cohen and Maireid Cohen, co-Administrators; Lucinda Lee, Interested Party.

The court and Ms. Hinshaw discuss the pending Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) with Ms. Hinshaw offering an update regarding the delay in fling the Final I&A. Ms. Hinshaw reports that the probate referee has the I&A. The court and Ms. Hinshaw discuss the language in the verification of her response to the Order to Show Cause. Ms. Hinshaw advises that she has used that language for many years and that it has never been an issue. The court advises Ms. Hinshaw that the verification language remains an issue for the court. The court advises of the several issues with this matter and that its tentative ruling is to remove the co-Administrators and order them to file an accounting and appoint a professional fiduciary. Ms. Hinshaw advises that this matter is almost ready to close and that removing the co-Administrators would delay the matter. The court advises that it is not inclined to appoint Ms. Lee as Personal Representative. Ms. Lee advises regarding her history with the co-Administrators.

After additional discussion, the court confirms that the co-Administrators remain suspended. The Order to Show Cause is taken under submission. The Status of Filing the Final Inventory and Appraisal is continued to Sep-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jun-13-2022 Continued To Jul-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, via Zoom, for Emily Cohen and Maireid Cohen, Co-Administrators.

The court points to the last calling of this matter and of the court's order that a bond in the amount of $15 million dollars be filed by Jun-10-2022 or the court would suspend the Co-Administrators. The court notes that the bond has not been filed so on its own motion, the court suspends the Co-Administrators Emily Cohen and Maireid Cohen. Ms. Hinshaw offers testimony regarding the actual value of the estate and the related the bond issue and asks that the court allow her clients to remain as Administrators. The court advises of the several issues regarding this matter and since those matters have not been resolved, the court reconfirms its order to suspend her clients. The court advises that it is setting an Order to Show Cause as to why the court should not remove her clients as Co-Administrators on Jul-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Status on Filing the Final Inventory and Appraisal is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jun-06-2022 Continued To Jun-13-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, via Zoom, for Emily Cohen and Maireid Cohen, Personal Representatives.

The court confirms with counsel that the Personal Representatives sold the 18th Street property without court confirmation and queries counsel as to why a petition to increase the bond was not filed immediately afterwards. Counsel reports that she was unaware that the property was owned by the LLC and it did not occur to her to file a petition to increase the bond. Counsel reports that the 706 value needed to be determined before the Inventory and Appraisal ("I&A") could be filed but that the value has now has been determined and the I&A will be filed soon. Counsel reports that the gross value of the estate is more than $19 million dollars and that her clients have access to only half of that amount. The court advises that her clients are extremely under bonded and that the court is considering suspending her clients and appointing a professional. Counsel offers oral testimony in support of the court not removing her clients.

After additional discussion, the court orders that a bond in the amount of $15 million dollars be filed by this Friday, Jun-10-2022, or the court will proceed with suspending the current Personal Representatives and appointing a professional fiduciary.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

***After hearing (Jun-06-2022): upon further consideration by the court, the matter is continued to Jun-13-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Apr-04-2022 Continued To Jun-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, via Zoom, for Emilly Cohen and Maireid Cohen, Co-Administrators.

Ms. Hinshaw reports that the main issue in valuating the estate are stocks and that the companies that hold said assets had previously been unwilling to provide asset information, but they have recently changed course and have recently agreed to assist with this matter. Ms. Hinshaw requests a 60-day continuance. After additional discussion, the court points to its concern regarding whether or not the matter is adequately bonded.

The Status on Filing the Final Inventory and Appraisal is continued to Jun-06-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Continued To Apr-04-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, via zoom, for Emily and Maereid Cohen, Co-Administrators.

Ms. Hinshaw reports that she has just received confirmation from one of the larger stock companies that they will be providing information after having refused to do so for some time. Ms. Hinshaw confirms that a completed Inventory and Appraisal is with her clients for signature and will be forwarded to the probate referee once she receives it. The court advises Ms. Hinshaw of its concern regarding whether her clients are properly bonded, and the court queries her regarding the current value of the estate. Ms. Hinshaw advises that she is unable to confirm the preceding at this time.

After additional discussion, the Status on Filing the Final Inventory and Appraisal is continued to Apr-04-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Teebay. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Caroline Hinsahw appeared by zzom for Emily Cohen and Maireid Cohen.

Ms. Hinshaw stated that the federal state tax return application for extension was filed at the end of November, that she has a good summary from JP Morgan which is holding a lot of the funds, and that JP Morgan has been very cooperative, but the people who are actually holding the assets are not being cooperative and are not providing information. Ms. Hinshaw also stated that they requested copies of all of the fidelity account statements covering date of death, that the statements do not tell them who the beneficiaries are or who the pay on death beneficiaries might be on those accounts, that she doesn't know if they should be included in the inventory and that she needs more time to find those things out. In addition, Ms. Hinshaw stated that they sold the real property, spoke of money that was borrowed and paid, spoke of the restricted stocks, stated that she can identify the source of the $3,000,000.00 tax payment, and spoke of the administrators having trusts that were left to them personally with independent trustees who are people that she doesn't know. Furthermore, Ms. Hinshaw stated that the administrators are working very hard and are diligent, that it has been very frustrating for the administrators, that there is a confidential marital settlement agreement which list in detail the assets of the estate, that the administrators are the beneficiaries and have waived bond, and that there are very small creditors who will be paid. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is continued to March 21, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jan-24-2022 Continued To Feb-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw, via ZOOM, for Emily Cohen and Maireid Cohen, Co-Administrators.

The sale was confirmed at $1,100,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). The court and counsel discuss whether to require a bond or order the proceeds from the sale be deposited into a blocked account. Ms. Hinshaw reports that waivers of bond have been filed and asks the court to waive the requirement of a bond. The court advises that it would prefer that a bond be filed or order the deposit of the proceeds in to a blocked account. Ms. Hinshaw advises that she would prefer to obtain a bond. Counsel is instructed to contact the assigned Examiner to discuss the bond amount which is to be determined by the proceeds from the sale of the real property.

The Report of Sale is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody (via ZOOM). Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-13-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw appeared by zoom for and with Emily Cohen by zoom and Maireid Cohen by zoom.

The Court asked about Emily's traveling plans. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Emily continues to maintain residency in San Francisco, that Emily will continue traveling, that Emily can be bonded for a minimal amount and that they have consulted with a professional fiduciary to assist. The court stated that it is considering appointment with bond and with limited authority. Ms. Hinshaw stated that limited authority is not the best idea with regard to real estate as it would require court confirmation at the time of sale which may affect the sale value of the property. The Court noted that sales that have taken place in court even recently have sold for well above the asking amount. Ms. Hinshaw stated that these ladies are very well qualified, that Emily has assured her that she can be in San Francisco on very short notice, that they have a professional accountant and that the property is in San Francisco. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission for the court to put together an order that sets bond at $50,000.00 and limited authority on the real property.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
